behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at a module boundary: summary-write path must read persisted per-dimension findings for that round before allowing PASS. Unit-only validation on an input array can miss repository/query wiring bugs. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text is limited to stored findings and summary storage (no real OS/network/subprocess, no model-output dependence, no multi-agent sequence).
behaviors.test_description: Chosen-level candidate (Integration): set up round R with persisted findings including one FAIL; call summary-store service with PASS for R; assert write is rejected and reason references failing dimensions. Adjacent lower candidate (Unit): call pure validator with in-memory findings and assert false; catches logic bug but misses wrong DB query/round filter bugs.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is incorrect data-source selection across modules (gate service + findings store + summary store). A pure function test cannot prove the gate actually queries findings storage. Higher levels disqualified by behavior text: it says computation reads stored findings; no OS/network/subprocess requirement, no LLM judgment quality, no multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Chosen-level candidate (Integration): persist summary text claiming all PASS but persist one dimension FAIL; run stop-gate computation; assert verdict is FAIL/block based on findings store. Adjacent lower candidate (Unit): test verdict function with provided findings object; would pass even if production code never fetches findings from storage.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: This is cross-module policy enforcement (request flags/authorization path + persistence of audit warning). Unit tests miss missing audit-write or incorrect wiring between override path and audit log sink. Higher levels rejected because behavior text specifies flag + auditable record, not real external infra, not model-output correctness, and not multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Chosen-level candidate (Integration): submit manual summary override without flag and with flag in two calls; assert first is rejected, second succeeds and creates warning audit record linked to actor/round. Adjacent lower candidate (Unit): test only `requiresOverrideFlag()`; cannot catch missing audit record emission.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is state handoff between fix-commit tracking and review-round metadata inside the gate. In-process module wiring with repository fakes/DB is sufficient to catch stale-round acceptance. System was considered but rejected because behavior text asks for identifier ordering after latest fix commit, not real git subprocess/OS semantics explicitly.
behaviors.test_description: Chosen-level candidate (Integration): persist latest fix commit C2 and a review round tied to older commit C1; attempt PASS finalize; assert blocked until round id tied to post-C2 review exists. Adjacent higher candidate (System): run against real git commits and hook scripts; catches git-integration faults but not required for this stated ordering rule.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior validates orchestration/freshness gating around an agent run (updated diff must have a newer round record) rather than evaluating model output quality. Per LLM-DEP rule, this can be Integration when exclusively testing infrastructure around the call. Agentic was considered and rejected because text requires a fresh round run event on updated diff, not correctness of what the model concluded.
behaviors.test_description: Chosen-level candidate (Integration): simulate live loop state with diff updated after fix commit, but no new review-round completion event; assert PASS finalize is blocked; then add fresh round metadata and assert unblocked. Adjacent higher candidate (Agentic): execute real model call and assert behavior; adds nondeterminism but does not increase detection power for this freshness gate failure mode.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is deterministic policy across stored results and round counters (gate + findings store + escalation emitter). This is module interaction, not single-function only. Higher levels rejected: behavior is about stored unresolved MUST-FIX findings and exhaustion state, with no explicit real OS/network dependency, no model-output quality assertion, and no required full live multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Chosen-level candidate (Integration): persist unresolved MUST-FIX findings and set rounds_used == rounds_limit; run finalization; assert escalation outcome and no PASS state transition. Adjacent lower candidate (Unit): evaluate `decideOutcome()` on synthetic struct; misses persistence/query mismatch and escalation side-effect wiring.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a live multi-round review workflow and end-to-end sequencing across rounds, unresolved findings carryover, exhaustion, gate block, and escalation emission. This crosses multiple agentic stages in sequence, so Workflow is the minimum real failure boundary. Lower levels were rejected because the quoted text 'live multi-round review workflow' disqualifies isolated Integration/Agentic tests that do not exercise full sequential handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Chosen-level candidate (Workflow): run a full review pipeline over multiple rounds on updated diffs where MUST-FIX persists; exhaust round budget; assert PASS remains blocked and escalation event is emitted at terminal state. Adjacent lower candidate (Agentic): single real model round plus gate check; can miss cross-round carryover/exhaustion bugs that only appear after sequential pipeline transitions.
